# bitstamp-python-client

Python package to communicate with the bitstamp.net API (v1 and v2).

Compatible with Python 2.7+ and Python 3.3+

## Overview

There are two classes. One for the public part of API and a second for the
trading part.

Public class doesn't need user credentials, because API commands which this
class implements are not bound to bitstamp user account.

Description of API: <https://www.bitstamp.net/api/>

## Install

Install from PyPi:

```
pip install BitstampClient
```

Install from git:

```
pip install git+git://github.com/kmadac/bitstamp-python-client.git
```

## Usage

Here's a quick example of usage:

```
>>> import bitstamp.client

>>> public_client = bitstamp.client.Public()
>>> print(public_client.ticker()['volume'])
8700.01208078

>>> trading_client = bitstamp.client.Trading(
...     username='999999', key='xxx', secret='xxx')
>>> print(trading_client.account_balance()['fee'])
0.5000
>>> print(trading_client.ticker()['volume'])   # Can access public methods
8700.01208078
```

## How to activate a new API key

1. Login your Bitstamp account
2. Click on Security -> Api Access
3. Select permissions which you want to have for you access key (if you don't
   check any box, you will get error message 'No permission found' after each
   API call)
4. Click the 'Generate key' button and don't forget to write down your Secret!
5. Click 'Activate'
6. Goto your Inbox and click on link sent by Bitstamp to activate this API key

```{image} https://raw.github.com/kmadac/bitstamp-python-client/master/class_diagram.png
:align: center
:alt: Class diagram
```

classes.name: bitstamp.client.TransRange
documentation:
Enum like object used in transaction method to specify time range
from which to get list of transactions

classes.name: bitstamp.client.BaseClient

methods.name: bitstamp.client.BaseClient._get
documentation: Make a GET request.

methods.name: bitstamp.client.BaseClient._post
documentation: Make a POST request.

methods.name: bitstamp.client.BaseClient._default_data
documentation: Default data for a POST request.

methods.name: bitstamp.client.BaseClient._construct_url
documentation: Adds the orderbook to the url if base and quote are specified.

methods.name: bitstamp.client.BaseClient._request
documentation:
Make a generic request, adding in any proxy defined by the instance.

Raises a `requests.HTTPError` if the response status isn't 200, and
raises a {class}`BitstampError` if the response contains a json encoded
error message.

A base class for the API Client methods that handles interaction with
the requests library.

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Public.ticker
documentation: Returns dictionary.

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Public.ticker_hour
documentation: Returns dictionary of the average ticker of the past hour.

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Public.order_book
documentation:
Returns dictionary with "bids" and "asks".

Each is a list of open orders and each order is represented as a list
of price and amount.

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Public.ohlc
documentation:
Returns Open, High, Low and close.
The parameter 'step' gives the interval of time in seconds for which data is retrieved.
If none from start or end timestamps are posted then endpoint returns
OHLC data to current unixtime. If both start and end timestamps are
posted, end timestamp will be used.

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Public.transactions
documentation:
Returns transactions for the last 'timedelta' seconds.
Parameter time is specified by one of two values of TransRange class.

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Public.conversion_rate_usd_eur
documentation:
Returns simple dictionary:

```
{'buy': 'buy conversion rate', 'sell': 'sell conversion rate'}
```

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Public.trading_pairs_info
documentation:
Returns list of dictionaries specifying details of each available trading pair:

```
{
    'description':'Litecoin / U.S. dollar',
    'name':'LTC/USD',
    'url_symbol':'ltcusd',
    'trading':'Enabled',
    'minimum_order':'5.0 USD',
    'counter_decimals':2,
    'base_decimals':8
},
```

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Trading.__init__
documentation:
Stores the username, key, and secret which is used when making POST
requests to Bitstamp.

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Trading.get_nonce
documentation:
Get a unique nonce for the bitstamp API.

This integer must always be increasing, so use the current unix time.
Every time this variable is requested, it automatically increments to
allow for more than one API request per second.

This isn't a thread-safe function however, so you should only rely on a
single thread if you have a high level of concurrent API requests in
your application.

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Trading._default_data
documentation:
Generate a one-time signature and other data required to send a secure
POST request to the Bitstamp API.

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Trading._expect_true
documentation:
A shortcut that raises a {class}`BitstampError` if the response didn't
just contain the text 'true'.

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Trading.account_balance
documentation:
Returns dictionary:

```
{u'btc_reserved': u'0',
 u'fee': u'0.5000',
 u'btc_available': u'2.30856098',
 u'usd_reserved': u'0',
 u'btc_balance': u'2.30856098',
 u'usd_balance': u'114.64',
 u'usd_available': u'114.64',
 ---If base and quote were specified:
 u'fee': u'',
 ---If base and quote were not specified:
 u'btcusd_fee': u'0.25',
 u'btceur_fee': u'0.25',
 u'eurusd_fee': u'0.20',
 }
There could be reasons to set base and quote to None (or False),
because the result then will contain the fees for all currency pairs
For backwards compatibility this can not be the default however.
```

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Trading.user_transactions
documentation:
Returns descending list of transactions. Every transaction (dictionary)
contains:

```
{u'usd': u'-39.25',
 u'datetime': u'2013-03-26 18:49:13',
 u'fee': u'0.20',
 u'btc': u'0.50000000',
 u'type': 2,
 u'id': 213642}
```

Instead of the keys btc and usd, it can contain other currency codes

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Trading.open_orders
documentation:
Returns JSON list of open orders. Each order is represented as a
dictionary.

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Trading.all_open_orders
documentation:
Returns JSON list of open orders of all currency pairs.
Each order is represented as a dictionary.

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Trading.order_status
documentation:
Returns dictionary.
- status: 'Finished'
  or      'In Queue'
  or      'Open'
- transactions: list of transactions
  Each transaction is a dictionary with the following keys:
      btc, usd, price, type, fee, datetime, tid
  or  btc, eur, ....
  or  eur, usd, ....

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Trading.cancel_order
documentation:
Cancel the order specified by order_id.

Version 1 (default for backwards compatibility reasons):
Returns True if order was successfully canceled, otherwise
raise a BitstampError.

Version 2:
Returns dictionary of the canceled order, containing the keys:
id, type, price, amount

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Trading.cancel_all_orders
documentation:
Cancel all open orders.

Returns True if it was successful, otherwise raises a
BitstampError.

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Trading.buy_limit_order
documentation: Order to buy amount of bitcoins for specified price.

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Trading.buy_market_order
documentation: Order to buy amount of bitcoins for market price.

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Trading.sell_limit_order
documentation: Order to sell amount of bitcoins for specified price.

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Trading.sell_market_order
documentation: Order to sell amount of bitcoins for market price.

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Trading.check_bitstamp_code
documentation:
Returns JSON dictionary containing USD and BTC amount included in given
bitstamp code.

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Trading.redeem_bitstamp_code
documentation:
Returns JSON dictionary containing USD and BTC amount added to user's
account.

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Trading.withdrawal_requests
documentation:
Returns list of withdrawal requests.

Each request is represented as a dictionary.

By default, the last 24 hours (86400 seconds) are returned.

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Trading.bitcoin_withdrawal
documentation: Send bitcoins to another bitcoin wallet specified by address.

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Trading.bitcoin_deposit_address
documentation: Returns bitcoin deposit address as unicode string

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Trading.unconfirmed_bitcoin_deposits
documentation:
Returns JSON list of unconfirmed bitcoin transactions.

Each transaction is represented as dictionary:

amount
  bitcoin amount
address
  deposit address used
confirmations
  number of confirmations

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Trading.litecoin_withdrawal
documentation: Send litecoins to another litecoin wallet specified by address.

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Trading.litecoin_deposit_address
documentation: Returns litecoin deposit address as unicode string

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Trading.ethereum_withdrawal
documentation: Send ethers to another ether wallet specified by address.

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Trading.ethereum_deposit_address
documentation: Returns ethereum deposit address as unicode string

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Trading.ripple_withdrawal
documentation: Returns true if successful.

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Trading.ripple_deposit_address
documentation: Returns ripple deposit address as unicode string.

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Trading.xrp_withdrawal
documentation: Sends xrps to another xrp wallet specified by address. Returns withdrawal id.

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Trading.xrp_deposit_address
documentation:
Returns ripple deposit address and destination tag as dictionary.
Example: {u'destination_tag': 53965834, u'address': u'rDsbeamaa4FFwbQTJp9Rs84Q56vCiWCaBx'}

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Trading.bch_withdrawal
documentation: Send bitcoin cash to another bitcoin cash wallet specified by address.

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Trading.bch_deposit_address
documentation: Returns bitcoin cash deposit address as unicode string

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Trading.transfer_to_main
documentation:
Returns dictionary with status.
subaccount has to be the numerical id of the subaccount, not the name

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Trading.transfer_from_main
documentation:
Returns dictionary with status.
subaccount has to be the numerical id of the subaccount, not the name

methods.name: bitstamp.client.Trading.websockets_token
documentation: Generates token required for subscribing to private WebSocket channels.

classes.name: bitstamp.client.BackwardsCompat

methods.name: bitstamp.client.BackwardsCompat.__init__
documentation: Instantiate the wrapped class.

methods.name: bitstamp.client.BackwardsCompat.__getattr__
documentation:
Return the wrapped attribute. If it's a callable then return the error
tuple when appropriate.

Version 1 used lower case class names that didn't raise an exception when
Bitstamp returned a response indicating an error had occured.

Instead, it returned a tuple containing `(False, 'The error message')`.
Backwards compatibility

classes.name: bitstamp.client.public
documentation: Deprecated version 1 client. Use {class}`Public` instead.

classes.name: bitstamp.client.trading
documentation: Deprecated version 1 client. Use {class}`Trading` instead.
